title: Welcome to FNCE 3030
content:
Welcome to FNCE 3030

Hi there,

 

Welcome to my Investments class!

 

The course provides the foundation for understanding financial assets and how to use them to allocate capital optimally over time. Emphasis will be placed on real-world applications of concepts/theories discussed in class. The course is particularly important for anyone pursuing a career in finance. Topics covered include financial markets and main asset classes; security issuance and the supply of financial assets; financial intermediaries and the mechanics of trading; capital preservation and the optimal portfolio allocation paradigm; distribution of returns and risk premium; solving the optimal portfolio allocation problem; one fund separation result; the capital asset pricing model; the arbitrage pricing theory; multi-factor models of risk and returns; the efficient market hypothesis and event studies; behavioral finance and elements of technical analysis; machine learning models; fixed income securities; stock picking, macro and industry analysis, equity valuation models, financial statement analysis, some elements of the theory of active portfolio management. 

 

The class will involve a fair amount of data work, which we will do in Excel, primarily. Our data will come from WRDS, which is the premier provider of data for academics/industry.
contentText:
Welcome to FNCE 3030
  
  
  
    Hi there,
 
Welcome to my Investments class!
 
The course provides the foundation for understanding financial assets and how to use them to allocate capital optimally over time. Emphasis will be placed on real-world applications of concepts/theories discussed in class. The course is particularly important for anyone pursuing a career in finance. Topics covered include financial markets and main asset classes; security issuance and the supply of financial assets; financial intermediaries and the mechanics of trading; capital preservation and the optimal portfolio allocation paradigm; distribution of returns and risk premium; solving the optimal portfolio allocation problem; one fund separation result; the capital asset pricing model; the arbitrage pricing theory; multi-factor models of risk and returns; the efficient market hypothesis and event studies; behavioral finance and elements of technical analysis; machine learning models; fixed income securities; stock picking, macro and industry analysis, equity valuation models, financial statement analysis, some elements of the theory of active portfolio management. 
 
The class will involve a fair amount of data work, which we will do in Excel, primarily. Our data will come from WRDS, which is the premier provider of data for academics/industry.
